info.description: This is the API definition for the Adshot Core service.
info.title: API of the Adshot Core service

get.description:
List / search through all Gamer profiles. This API endpoint uses the OData query language as described in this Microsoft API guidelines document for filtering, pagination and sorting.

There are several Nodejs libraries available providing these query operations out of the box (eg: https://github.com/TossShinHwa/node-odata)


This endpoint can only be called by users with the role 'admin'.

parameters.name: $filter
parameters.description:
The filter querystring parameter allows clients to filter a collection of resources that are addressed by a request URL. The expression specified with filter is evaluated for each resource in the collection, and only items where the expression evaluates to true are included in the response. Resources for which the expression evaluates to false or to null, or which reference properties that are unavailable due to permissions, are omitted from the response.

Example return all gamers with country equal to Belgium

GET /v1/gamers?filter=country eq belgium


The value of the filter option is a Boolean expression.

Services that support filter SHOULD support the following minimal set of operations.

Comparison operators
eqEqualsubject eq 'Redmond'
neNot equalcity ne 'London'
gtGreater thanprice gt 10
geGreater than or equalprice ge 20
ltLess thanprice lt 10
leLess than or equalprice le 20
Logical operators
andLogical andprice le 200 and price gt 3.5
orLogical orprice le 3.5 or price gt 200
notLogical negationnot price le 3.5
Grouping operators
( )Precedence grouping(priority eq 1 or city eq 'Redmond') and price gt 100

parameters.name: $top
parameters.description:
Clients MAY use $top and $skip query parameters to specify a number of results to return and an offset into the collection. When both $top and $skip are given by a client, the server SHOULD first apply $skip and then $top on the collection.

GET /v1/gamers?$top=5&skip=2

parameters.name: $orderBy
parameters.description:
The value of the orderBy parameter contains a comma-separated list of expressions used to sort the items.

The expression MAY include the suffix "asc" for ascending or "desc" for descending, separated from the property name by one or more spaces. If "asc" or "desc" is not specified, the service MUST order by the specified property in ascending order.

Items MUST be sorted by the result values of the first expression, and then items with the same value for the first expression are sorted by the result value of the second expression, and so on. The sort order is the inherent order for the type of the property.

Example: return all gamers sorted by firstName in ascending order

GET /v1/gamers?orderBy=firstName asc

parameters.name: $select
parameters.description:
A data service URI with a $select query option identifies the same set of Entries as a URI without a $select query option; however, the value of $select specifies that the response body should only include a subset of the properties which would have been returned had the URI not included a $select query option.

Example: retrieve a list of all gamers including only the userName and country properties.

GET /v1/gamers?select=userName,country

get.summary: Retrieve details of a specific Gamer.
get.description: Retrieve the details of a specific Gamer. When the authenticated user is not the owner of this Gamer resource (and does not have role 'admin'), a stripped down version of the GamerDto is returned, leaving out sensitive properties such as iban, address information, etc.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: id

patch.description: Update a Gamer profile. Users without the 'admin' role are only allowed to update their own Gamer resource. Fields that are not included in the request body will remain unchanged as described in the Microsoft API guidelines: https://github.com/layeredprints/api-guidelines/blob/vNext/Guidelines.md#742-patch

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: id

get.summary: Retrieve the Gamer resource owned by the authenticated user.
get.description: Retrieve the Gamer resource owned by the authenticated user. The userId should be parsed from the JWT token in the Authentication header and should match the ownerId of the Gamer resource

patch.summary: Update Gamer profile owned by the authenticated user.
patch.description: Update a Gamer profile. The userId should be parsed from the JWT token in the Authentication header and should match the ownerId of the Gamer resource. Fields that are not included in the request body will remain unchanged as described in the Microsoft API guidelines: https://github.com/layeredprints/api-guidelines/blob/vNext/Guidelines.md#742-patch

get.summary: List / search through all Advertiser profiles
get.description:
List / search through all Advertiser profiles. This API endpoint uses the OData query language as described in this Microsoft API guidelines document for filtering, pagination and sorting.

There are several Nodejs libraries available providing these query operations out of the box (eg: https://github.com/TossShinHwa/node-odata)


This endpoint can only be called by users with the role 'admin'.

parameters.description:

The expression specified with filter is evaluated for each resource in the collection, and only items where the expression evaluates to true are included in the response. Resources for which the expression evaluates to false or to null, or which reference properties that are unavailable due to permissions, are omitted from the response.

parameters.name: $orderBy

The expression MAY include the suffix "asc" for ascending or "desc" for descending, separated from the property name by one or more spaces. If "asc" or "desc" is not specified, the service MUST order by the specified property in ascending order.

Items MUST be sorted by the result values of the first expression, and then items with the same value for the first expression are sorted by the result value of the second expression, and so on. The sort order is the inherent order for the type of the property.

parameters.description:
A data service URI with a $select query option identifies the same set of Entries as a URI without a $select query option; however, the value of $select specifies that the response body should only include a subset of the properties which would have been returned had the URI not included a $select query option.

get.summary: Retrieve details of a specific Advertiser
get.description: Retrieve the details of an Advertiser. When the authenticated user does not own the Advertiser resource and does not have the role 'admin', a stripped down version of this resource excluding sensitive properties is returned.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: id

patch.description: Update a specific Advertiser. Only the user owning the Advertiser resource and admin users are allowed to call this endpoint.

get.summary: Retrieve the Advertiser resource owned by the authenticated user.

get.description:
List / search through all Campaigns owned by the signed in user. Users with the role 'admin' will receive all Campaigns. This API endpoint uses the OData query language as described in this Microsoft API guidelines document for filtering, pagination and sorting.

There are several Nodejs libraries available providing these query operations out of the box (eg: https://github.com/TossShinHwa/node-odata)

parameters.name: $filter
parameters.description:
The filter querystring parameter allows clients to filter a collection of resources that are addressed by a request URL. The expression specified with filter is evaluated for each resource in the collection, and only items where the expression evaluates to true are included in the response. Resources for which the expression evaluates to false or to null, or which reference properties that are unavailable due to permissions, are omitted from the response.

Example return all campaigns that have not ended yet

GET /v1/campaigns?filter=status ne ended


The value of the filter option is a Boolean expression.

A data service URI with a $select query option identifies the same set of Entries as a URI without a $select query option; however, the value of $select specifies that the response body should only include a subset of the properties which would have been returned had the URI not included a $select query option.

delete.description: Delete a Campaign. All child entities will be removed from the database as well. If the Campaign was already launched, it will not be removed from the database, but instead it's status (and the status of all child resources) will change to 'canceled'.

parameters.description: id

All child MeasurableServiceRequest entities will be removed from the database as well (or have their status changed to 'canceled' if the campaign was already launched.)

get.description:

There are several Nodejs libraries available providing these query operations out of the box (eg: https://github.com/TossShinHwa/node-odata)

Example return all sponsorships that are in the 'offered' status with category 'exclusive'

The value of the filter option is a Boolean expression.

get.description:
List / search through all Channels owned by the signed in user. Users with the 'admin' role will receive all Channels. This API endpoint uses the OData query language as described in this Microsoft API guidelines document for filtering, pagination and sorting.

There are several Nodejs libraries available providing these query operations out of the box (eg: https://github.com/TossShinHwa/node-odata)

The filter querystring parameter allows clients to filter a collection of resources that are addressed by a request URL. The expression specified with filter is evaluated for each resource in the collection, and only items where the expression evaluates to true are included in the response. Resources for which the expression evaluates to false or to null, or which reference properties that are unavailable due to permissions, are omitted from the response.

Example return all youtube channels

The value of the filter option is a Boolean expression.

Services that support filter SHOULD support the following minimal set of operations.

parameters.name: $orderBy
parameters.description:
The value of the orderBy parameter contains a comma-separated list of expressions used to sort the items.

The expression MAY include the suffix "asc" for ascending or "desc" for descending, separated from the property name by one or more spaces. If "asc" or "desc" is not specified, the service MUST order by the specified property in ascending order.

Items MUST be sorted by the result values of the first expression, and then items with the same value for the first expression are sorted by the result value of the second expression, and so on. The sort order is the inherent order for the type of the property.

A data service URI with a $select query option identifies the same set of Entries as a URI without a $select query option; however, the value of $select specifies that the response body should only include a subset of the properties which would have been returned had the URI not included a $select query option.

Example: retrieve a list of all channels including only the type and url properties.
